hypothesis: Adding a '→ [agent]' routing suggestion to each actionable-now finding (e.g., '→ dev', '→ seo', '→ analyst', '→ boss-decision') eliminates boss's triage overhead and increases adoptions above 9/week. Evidence: Cycle 3 KEEP (9 adoptions) confirmed tier classification primes action; the remaining friction is boss deciding which agent receives each item. Pre-routing removes that decision step. Cycle 4 runs post-Reyco sprint in a cleaner operational tempo, removing the sprint-mode confound from Cycle 3.
changes_description: Updated ecosystem_scan_adoption surface to cycle 4 format: added explicit → [agent] routing tags to each actionable-now finding (→ dev, → seo, → analyst, → boss-decision). Boss receives fleet-level digest; specialists receive targeted send-message per domain finding. Channel post mirrors tiered classification for paper trail. Change from cycle 3: routing destination now pre-specified per item, eliminating boss triage overhead of deciding which agent receives each signal.
